risk_lines_for_manual_review.text: Status: **bounded draft for internal review; not final submission-ready**

This work is positioned as an audit and policy-handoff contribution rather than a claim that one new detector solves all floorplan recognition tasks. The key distinction is the runtime contract: target-level model-output

We organize evaluation into three explicitly separated modes. The first mode is **target-level model-output evaluation**, where predicted boxes or masks are compared against locked target annotations. This mode supports

Second, the current evidence does not establish full end-to-end raster scene-graph quality. Symbol target-level metrics, image-only family box metrics, and expected-json relation replay are separate evidence modes. They

Fifth, current real-world/industrial generalization is not established. Existing real-raster annotation work is pilot-level and needs human review before it can support claims about industrial drawings or deployment beyo

For relations, the expected-json contains policy should be evaluated on model-output graphs after upstream visual predictions are frozen. This would measure whether relation improvements survive realistic detector noise.
- Unsafe: claim full end-to-end raster scene-graph robustness.
- Do not claim: full raster scene-graph solved.

conclusion: No blocking overclaim found in automated scan. Risk terms appear in caveat/blocked-claim contexts; keep them during final human review.
recommended_edits_before_handoff: Replace generic citation placeholders with verified references or keep as explicit TODOs.
Draw Figure 1 and optionally Figure 2 from P131 plan.
If page-limited, move pixel mask and selector bottleneck tables to appendix.
